heading: Corps budgeting; project deauthorizations; comprehensive backlog report
label: 579a Corps budgeting; project deauthorizations; comprehensive backlog report

(b)(2). , which directed the substitution of “such period” for “such 30 month period” in last sentence, was executed by making the substitution for “such 30-month period” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. (b)(2). inserted after first sentence “Before submission of such list to Congress, the Secretary shall notify each Senator in whose State, and each Member of the House of Representatives in whose district, a project (including any part thereof) on such list would be located.”Pub. Savings Clause, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–270, title I, § 1332(b)132 Stat. 3835Oct. 23, 2018“Notwithstanding the third sentence of section 1001(b)(2) of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (former ), projects and separable elements of projects identified in the fiscal year 2017 report prepared in accordance with such section and submitted to Congress on , shall not be deauthorized unless such projects and separable elements meet the requirements of section 1301(b)(1)(A) of the Water Resources Development Act of 2016 () [former ].”33 U.S.C. 579a(b)(2)130 Stat. 168733 U.S.C. 579d(b)(1)(A)December 15, 2016

(a) and (c) of this section applied to projects authorized for construction by (see Short Title of 1988 Amendment note set out under ), except that the 5-year period during which funds had to be obligated to prevent deauthorization began on , and were also to apply to projects authorized for construction subsequent to , except that 5-year period during which funds had to be obligated to prevent deauthorization began on the date of the authorization of such projects, was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: Funds to be obligated for construction to avoid deauthorization
children.label: a Funds to be obligated for construction to avoid deauthorization
children.text: Any project authorized for construction by this Act shall not be authorized after the last day of the 5-year period beginning on , unless during such period funds have been obligated for construction, including planning and designing, of such project.November 17, 1986

children.heading: Transmission to Congress of list of unconstructed projects or separable elements authorized but not receiving obligations during 10 fiscal years preceding transmission; two-year updates of list
children.label: b Transmission to Congress of list of unconstructed projects or separable elements authorized but not receiving obligations during 10 fiscal years preceding transmission; two-year updates of list

children.text: Not later than one year after , the Secretary shall transmit to Congress a list of unconstructed projects, or unconstructed separable elements of projects, which have been authorized, but have received no obligations during the 10 full fiscal years preceding the transmittal of such list. A project or separable element included in such list is not authorized after , if funds have not been obligated for construction of such project or element after , and before .November 17, 1986December 31, 1989November 17, 1986December 31, 1989

children.heading: Comprehensive construction backlog and operation and maintenance report.—
children.label: 2 Comprehensive construction backlog and operation and maintenance report.—
i
children.text: a complete list of all projects and separable elements of projects of the Corps of Engineers that are authorized for construction but have not been completed;

children.label: ii
children.text: a complete list of all feasibility studies of the Corps of Engineers that Congress has authorized the Secretary to carry out for which a Report of the Chief of Engineers has not been issued;

children.label: iii
children.text: a complete list of all environmental infrastructure projects authorized by Congress under section 219 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1992 (); and106 Stat. 4835

children.label: iv
children.text: a list of major Federal operation and maintenance needs of projects and properties under the control of the Corps of Engineers.

children.label: i
children.text: the date of authorization of the feasibility study, project, or separable element, including any subsequent modifications to the original authorization;

children.label: ii
children.text: the original budget authority for the feasibility study, project, or separable element;

children.label: iii
children.text: a brief description of the feasibility study, project, or separable element;

children.label: iv
children.text: the estimated date of completion of the feasibility study, project, or separable element, assuming all capability is fully funded;

children.label: v
children.text: the estimated total cost of completion of the feasibility study, project, or separable element;

children.label: vi
children.text: the amount of funds spent on the feasibility study, project, or separable element, including Federal and non-Federal funds;

children.label: vii
children.text: the amount of appropriations estimated to be required in each fiscal year during the period of construction to complete the project or separable element by the date specified under clause (iv);

children.label: viii
children.text: the location of the feasibility study, project, or separable element;

children.label: ix
children.text: a statement from the non-Federal interest for the project or separable element indicating the non-Federal interest’s capability to provide the required local cooperation estimated to be required for the project or separable element in each fiscal year during the period of construction;

children.label: x
children.text: the benefit-cost ratio of the project or separable element, calculated using the discount rate specified by the Office of Management and Budget for purposes of preparing the President’s budget pursuant to chapter 11 of title 31;

children.label: xi
children.text: the benefit-cost ratio of the project or separable element, calculated using the discount rate utilized by the Corps of Engineers for water resources development project planning pursuant to ; andsection 1962d–17 of title 42

children.label: xii
children.text: the last fiscal year in which the project or separable element incurred obligations.

children.label: i
children.text: the authority under which the project was authorized or the property was acquired by the Corps of Engineers;

children.label: ii
children.text: a brief description of the project or property;

children.label: iii
children.text: an estimate of the Federal costs to meet the major operation and maintenance needs at the project or property; and

children.label: iv
children.text: an estimate of unmet or deferred operation and maintenance needs at the project or property.

children.label: I
children.text: the Committee on Environment and Public Works and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.

children.heading: .—Public availability
children.label: ii .—Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make a copy of the lists available on a publicly accessible website site in a manner that is downloadable, searchable, and sortable.1So in original.1
